HMW-V06: FM Job Detail — Full Page

How might we lay out the complete FM job detail page — route timeline, inline driver assignment, cargo, fees, proofs, and activity log — so the dispatcher can plan, dispatch, and monitor a multi-leg FM job without switching pages?
This is the most complex page in the vendor app. An FM job with 3 legs, hub ops at intermediate stops, fees for reconciliation, proof documents, and a full activity log. The page needs to serve three moments: (1) Planning — adding stops and assigning drivers. (2) Monitoring — seeing where the cargo is right now. (3) Reconciliation — reviewing fees after completion. HMW-V05 designed the timeline in isolation. This mockup shows the FULL page with all sections.

State 1: Planning — Assigning Drivers

Interactive
The dispatcher just opened a new FM job. Route is planned (3 legs), two drivers assigned, one leg still needs a driver. This shows the inline assignment interaction: dropdowns inside the leg card for unassigned legs, confirmed state for assigned legs.
← My Jobs J01 FM Trucking FM DO-2603-001 · TikTok · MAWB 160-84329871

State 2: Execution — Live Monitoring

Live
Leg 1 is delivered. Shenzhen Hub is processing (ARR done, PAL in progress). Leg 2 is waiting. The timeline shows progress with completed legs in green, active hub in amber, future legs faded. Proof documents from leg 1's pickup and delivery are visible. Activity log shows the full chain of events.

29 Mar 16:00
Job created · Ops Admin

Full Page Layout: Route → Cargo → Fees → Proofs → Log

The section order for FM jobs is: Route Plan → Cargo → Fee Breakdown → Proofs → Activity Log.

This overrides the "fees-first" rule (HMW-V02) specifically for FM jobs. The dispatcher opens an FM job to plan or monitor the route, not to reconcile fees.

Inline driver assignment: Unassigned legs show driver + vehicle dropdowns directly inside the leg card. Same gray border as assigned legs, just with dropdowns instead of confirmed names. No special "needs driver" highlight, no amber, no extra chip. The empty dropdowns ARE the signal.

Cargo as one line: "24 bags · 1,240 kg · 8.4 CBM". Same in all states. Bag progress lives on the hub node, not duplicated in cargo.

No explanatory text: No "Read-only" notes, no "Your hub" labels, no "Inbound → Processing → Outbound" sub-text. The user knows their own system. Don't patronize.

No data we can't back up: No estimated arrival times unless we calculate them. Don't show "(est.)" if there's no estimation engine.
